description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: build, publish, and deploy, and define variables AZURE_RESOURCEGROUP, DOCKERREGISTRY, PLATFORMSETTINGSFILE, REPOSITORYNAME. In the build stage add a build_and_test job using the microsoft/dotnet:2.0.0-sdk image that runs `dotnet restore`, `dotnet build`, `dotnet test ./test/BasketServiceTests/BasketServiceTests.csproj`, `dotnet publish -c release`, and then changes to the publish folder and creates a tarball with `cd ./src/BasketService/bin/Release/netcoreapp2.0/publish && tar -zcvf ../../../../../../service.tar.gz .`; it stores service.tar.gz as an artifact that expires in 3 hours. In the publish stage add a publish_docker_image job that depends on build_and_test, runs on the docker:git image with a docker:dind service, extracts the tarball (`mkdir ./output` and `tar -zxvf service.tar.gz -C ./output`), builds a Docker image with `docker build -t $DOCKERREGISTRY/$REPOSITORYNAME:$(git rev-parse --short=8 HEAD) .`, logs in to $DOCKERREGISTRY using $AZURE_SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_USER and $AZURE_SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_PASSWORD, and pushes the image; this job runs only on the master branch. In the deploy stage add a deploy_to_staging job using the node:8.1.3 image, limited to the master branch, that sets SERVICE_VERSION and DOCKER_SERVICE_IMAGE environment variables from the current commit hash, makes deploy.sh executable, and runs `./deploy.sh`; it deploys to the GitLab environment named staging with URL https://staging.
